CONTACT US
Welcome to Revelectrik
Powering the Future

Revelectrik is Revolutionizing the future of Electric Transportation

Revelectrik is at the forefront of the next wave of electric mobility, pioneering a sustainable energy infrastructure tailored for commercial EV fleets. Our flagship innovation, a 2 kWh Solar Energy Collection and Charging System designed for electric school buses and medium duty commercial vehicles, directly addresses two critical global challenges; energy reliability and grid congestion during peak demand.

Unlike traditional solar add-ons, our system operates as an intelligent, bidirectional energy asset. Powered by our proprietary Solar Energy Management Controller (SEMC) and integrated with an AI enabled cloud telematics platform, it not only reduces charging losses and operational cost, but also feeds clean energy back to the grid during peak events, creating new revenue streams through vehicle to grid and energy monetization models.

Developed in strategic collaboration with Fermata Energy and Left Coast Engineering, early deployments have already demonstrated 3.65 megawatts of renewable energy generation capacity across scaled fleet scenarios, proving both technical feasibility and economic viability.

Revelectrik is now nearing its production scaling phase and is actively seeking vision aligned investors, OEM collaborators, and utility partners to establish a new industry standard for energy resilient, self powered fleet electrification.

The future of electric vehicles is not just electric. It is self generating, intelligent, and grid empowering. Join us in building it.

Revelectrik is Revolutionizing the future of Electric Transportation

Our flagship innovation, a 2 kWh Solar Energy Collection and Charging System designed for electric school buses and medium duty commercial vehicles, directly addresses two critical global challenges; energy reliability and grid congestion during peak demand.

Unlike traditional solar add-ons, our system operates as an intelligent, bidirectional energy asset. Subhasis Behera, a seasoned engineer with 15 years of experience in the automotive and energy domains, possesses deep expertise in Electric Vehicle (EV) systems, embedded software, Battery Management Systems (BMS), and Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) development. As the CEO & Chief Engineer of Revelectrik, he leads the company’s mission at the convergence of embodied AI, power electronics, and edge-intelligent systems, translating first-principles engineering into scalable, real-world solutions for commercial fleets and critical infrastructure.

At Revelectrik, Subhasis is spearheading a Department of Energy–aligned solar-to-commercial-vehicle charging initiative, architecting next-generation MPPT/LLC power conversion stages and adaptive control schemes to support sustainable, decentralized energy deployment. Complementing this work, he is also developing Zephyrus Horizon—a helium-based aerial system equipped with AI-driven control algorithms to harvest wind energy and atmospheric moisture from the lower troposphere (≈1–2 km altitude). The system employs a hybrid control framework that blends Model Predictive Control (MPC), neuro-adaptive feedback, and Embedded Policy Learning to continually refine control actions based on real-world environmental feedback—optimizing performance in uncertain and dynamic conditions (Patent Application filed).

Subhasis is also building a compact, field-deployable AI toolchain for edge inference, on-device adaptation, and edge-cloud-edge learning loops. This toolchain underpins a new paradigm of intelligent diagnostics and control; one that shifts from rule-based telemetry toward causal reasoning and continuous learning, enabling systems that adapt and evolve with their operational context. Drawing on experience across both combustion and electric platforms, Subhasis brings a rare ability to bridge classical control theory with cutting-edge AI, unlocking practical innovation in mobility, energy, and autonomous systems.

Subhasis Behera
CEO
and Chief Engineer
EXECUTIVE TEAM
Dr. Frank Lee advises Revelectrik as Senior Technical Advisor, bringing deep experience in advanced vehicle electrification, control systems, and systems engineering. Over his career, he has led cross-functional engineering teams on complex programs involving battery systems, vehicle controls, and model-based development, guiding projects from early concept through validation and production launch.
Dr. Lee is known for bridging rigorous engineering fundamentals with practical, production-ready solutions. He has worked across OEMs, suppliers, and technology-driven startups, shaping architectures for next-generation electric and intelligent vehicles while mentoring teams and building robust engineering processes.
At Revelectrik, Dr. Lee supports the team on high-level architecture decisions, controls and safety strategy, and how to scale prototype concepts into reliable, certifiable products that can succeed in real-world commercial deployment.
Dr. Frank Lee
Sr. Technical Advisor
Dr. Nishaki Mehta advises Revelectrik as Scientific Advisor, bringing deep experience at the intersection of cardiology, clinical practice, and medical-device innovation. She is a cardiac electrophysiologist at Corewell William Beaumont University Hospital and an Associate Professor of Medicine at Oakland University William Beaumont School of Medicine.
Beyond her clinical work, Dr. Mehta is actively involved in research and technology development, with a focus on improving how clinicians monitor, understand, and manage heart rhythm and cardiovascular health. She has contributed to multiple peer-reviewed publications and conference presentations and regularly collaborates with engineers and innovators on new tools for patient care.
She guides the Revelectrik team on how emerging technologies can be designed to fit seamlessly into everyday medical practice.

Dr. Nishaki Mehta
Scientific Advisor
Dr. Tony Zhou brings deep, real-world expertise in autonomous driving and ADAS to Revelectrik’s advisory board. Over a career spanning leadership roles at companies such as Lucid Motors, General Motors, FAW Group, and Phoenix EV, he has helped shape the evolution of L2-L4 autonomy, from system architecture and sensor fusion to localization and functional safety.
As former CTO of Phoenix EV, Dr. Zhou guided the development of next-generation autonomous bus platforms and advanced drive-by-wire systems, with a strong focus on safety, reliability, and real-world deployability. He is an active inventor with more than 18 patents in AI-driven localization, perception, and path planning.
In his role as advisor, Dr. Zhou supports Revelectrik on questions of autonomy strategy, system architecture, and how to bridge the gap between cutting-edge research and robust, production-ready platforms.
Dr. Tony Zhou
Sr. Technical Advisor
